title: Elders in Heaven Serving the Lord
summary: “And round about the throne were four and twenty seats: and upon the seats I saw four and twenty elders sitting, clothed in white raiment; and they had on their heads crowns of gold.” (Rev 4:4). The number 24 represents several things. For one, it points to the 12 representatives of the tribes of Israel along with the 12 Apostles. Hence a completion of the entire assembly of God from the new and old testaments. “And are built upon the foundation of the apostles and prophets, Jesus Christ himself being the chief corner stone” (Eph 2:20). In addition, the number 24 can also point to the Levitical order of service in the Temple structured by King David in I Chronicles 24:7-18. These priestly courses of service ensured there was service to the Lord 24/7. Incense, prayers, worship were offered to God on a continual basis. Hallelujah! The church has 24 elders who ensure continual service before the Lord Jesus in heaven. This is what the Apostle John found discovered and shared with us.
